name: Jalen Nailor
headline: Free-agent payday hands Nailor his first real volume
body:
Nailor's 2025 line tells the story of a buried but efficient field-stretcher: 53 targets on a 72.7% route share, 444 yards and four scores, with 12.38 air yards per target and a 17.5% air-yards share that outran his 11.3% target share. The efficiency was real too, a 0.198 EPA per play and 50.9% success rate, with 12 red-zone looks signaling more scoring upside than his snap count allowed. He simply never commanded the ball, posting a 0.292 WOPR and just 1.12 yards per route run.

Vegas paid him three years and $35 million to fix exactly that. With minimal receiver competition added and Nailor slotting as a primary perimeter option, the projected jump to roughly 42-566-4 is well within reach if his target rate climbs. He flashed top-seven weekly WR ceilings whenever he saw five-plus looks last year. At an ADP near 151, he's a sensible late dart with weekly WR3 equity.
